title: Nuclei Isolation from Frozen Tissue or Frozen hPCLS
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"3ndf5","text":"This protocol follows the \"Chromium Nuclei Isolation Kit\" guidelines for the process for isolating Nuclei from frozen tissues and/or PCLS (Precision-Cut Lung Slices) for use in compatible 10x Genomics Single Cell assays.","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Add lysis buffer ( 200 µL ) & dissociate with pestle until homogeneous while On ice .

Perform all protocol steps On ice and centrifugation steps at 4 °C . Transfer frozen tissue (∼ 50 mg ; use 2 slices if isolating from PCLS) to pre-chilled Sample Dissociation Tube (2000564) and place on wet ice.

Perform tissue dissociation On ice . Use one pestle per sample. DO NOT discard pestles until nuclei isolation process is complete. Add lysis buffer ( 300 µL ) and pipette mix 10×. If not homogeneous, continue to dissociate with the pestle until able to pipette mix.
Pipette dissociated tissue onto assembled and pre-chilled Nuclei Isolation Column and Collection Tube (2000562 & 2000563).
Vortex flowthrough in Collection Tube for 3200 rpm, , 10 seconds minimum to resuspend nuclei.
Resuspend nuclei in 1 mL wash and resuspension Buffer.
Resuspend pellet with debris removal buffer ( 500 µL ).
Resuspend nuclei pellet in 50 µL – 500 µL wash and resuspension Buffer.

Vortex nuclei for 00:00:03 and determine final nuclei concentration using AOPI or Ethidium Homodimer-1 fluorescent staining dyes and dilute if necessary for target nuclei load. Adjust nuclei concentration as necessary for intended downstream assay.
Vortex nuclei for 00:00:03 and keep samples On ice . Proceed immediately to 10× Genomics Single GEM Generation and Barcoding.
creator: Nayra Cardenes
